content: Just as linguistic reflection (the core meaning of reflexion taught in high school English) requires a learner to step back and evaluate their own writing against implicit communicative standards to improve clarity, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate graded, metalinguistic self-correction modeled on structured high school writing reflection exercises. This framing reframes LLM self-reflection not as a novel AI innovation, but as a guided pedagogical practice for large language models, where the LLM acts as its own writing tutor responding to inconsistent output perspectives.
